content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "String LCM". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/problemset/problem/1473/B
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T test cases follow. Each test case contains two strings s and t.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the shortest string that contains both s and t as a substring.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: To find the shortest string that contains both s and t as a substring, we can use the concept of the least common multiple (LCM) of two numbers. However, since we are dealing with strings, we need to find the LCM of the lengths of the two strings and then construct the resulting string.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * (length of s + length of t))
solution.spaceComplexity: O(T * (length of s + length of t))
